item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, this doesn't cut it for me for several reasons.
First, it seems like something that promotes the ACAB mentality and I've had enough of that bs. I really don't want to see that in this community as well.
Second, the secondary effect seems too over the top for me. I would've been ok with the remorse/guit (which could have been non-anomalous for extra effect), but the erratic behaviour and catatonia that follow the removal of the badge are too much.
Last, but not least, as someone already stated, being a psychopath or a sociopath don't really show as much as it's implied in the article. It can be said that the object desensitizes the wearers, but it doesn't make them psycho/sociopaths.
Overall, a firm -1 from me

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is bad. You put on the badge, everything is justified… The point about bad police is made very heavy-handedly, but it is also muddled. Why do they see everybody's actions as justified? This is more like they become completely desensitized… not just to violence, but anything.

Then the guilt and depression, it would happen to most people in that situation, nonanomalously, but it's still not really necessary to the article.

A bigger problem is, the experiments are so redundant. They just show how subjects saw everything as justified, then they got depressed. They repeat the descruption, and each other. And what is the big idea having these guys participate in mass murder? What is going on?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scip didn't work for me. There are a number of odd or incorrect word choices and statements in the article that stuck out to me such as "Foundation psychologists and speech pathologists have noticed that the speaking patterns of SCP-1339 subjects resemble those of psycho- and sociopaths.". As far as I'm aware most people on the antisocial personality disorder spectrum don't have obvious differences in their speech patterns. Most are fairly adept at blending in. Another example is the use of the word 'digressions'. I think this may have supposed to have been 'transgressions' but not sure. One of my other issues is with the lack of testing on people with APD. Seems a bit of a gap in the testing. Those concerns combined with my dislike of the tone of the article made me -1 it.

item.title: theory
item.content:encoded: So my theory (for it's secondary effect of immense guilt that is). Is that upon removal of the skip memories of some of the crimes people committed while wearing it are put(?) in your brain causing you to remember the crimes others committed while wearing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing is, this really doesn't get across as clearly. It doesn't turn you into a semi-obvious embodiment of the law, it turns you into an amoral person and then makes you feel super-guilty. I respect how it takes a concept and really runs with it, but it's a rather unnuanced concept, at least how it comes across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good and surprisingly undervoted. I hate writing that sentence, but I can't avoid it if it's the truth.

However, I really liked what some other guy wrote in the comments: taking off the badge makes you feel guilty of all the horrible stuff people representing the law haver ever done or will ever do. That's fascinating and horrifying and would really do wonders for the scip. As it stands now, I don't really understand who are they feeling guilty for (Criminals? the police? regular people? psychopaths?).

Upvoted anyways!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necroposting power activate!

No they don't. That's why they have to be sex offenders. The poor girl has to be traumatized for it to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for final addendum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i was about to say it should be used for montauk, then i remembered they have to be disgusted by what theyre doing in order for it to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty good. Only

those poor fuckers rest in peace.

I dunno if that, as the doctor's official note, as included in the report about a SCP, doesn't distrupt the clinical tone. IMO it's quite over the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it enough to forgive the tone problems. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fridge horror, right here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the basis of a few humans, a chimp, and a parakeet, some researcher has decided that it doesn't affect non-primates?

This is kind of the biological equivalent of saying "they couldn't hear the explosion in Honolulu, but they could in Manhattan and the Bronx; clearly it must have been audible east of the Mississppi." It might be true, but there's just not enough evidence cited to support drawing the line at that particular spot.

Drop the parakeet and just say "extensive testing with various species has shown the item only exhibits a discernible effect on hominids." ("Hominid" includes humans, chimps, bonobos, gorillas, and orangutans but excludes gibbons, monkeys, tarsiers and lemurs … all of which are primates.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't have to name an actual SCP, much less a famous one. Surely you can find something gruesome for your guy to do without naming those two articles out of the thousand SCPs we have, yes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, that wasn't my idea at all. In fact, I figured that subjects felt the guilt of all the worlds criminals. But that idea…that gave me the shivers. And it makes sense, even more do with the Foundation. Anybody who wore SCP-1339 would feel all the guilt of SCP staff of all time…

Sweet lord, I scared myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: exploring that could really add some depth to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's an implication I picked up with this that adds a little bit of extra horror, but it may or may not have been intended.

I got the feeling that one of the reasons people feel 'justified' while wearing it is because they feel they are, in a sense, the law.

Which made me think that the crimes they 'confessed' to… were committed by Law Enforcement personnel (or the time-appropriate equivalent).

Just added a little bit of horror to the whole thing, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you both, but I don't want to be one of those people who reference 682 or 231 in their first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anyone wishes to correct grammatical or syntax errors, please feel free to do so without running it by me, as long as the change doesn't alter the meaning of the sentence too radically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this. A few little bits are not quite the standard format for articles, but I liked it!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really, really long. That's fine in and of itself, but it doesn't really go anywhere other than confirming the description a few times. Ayup, that's what it does alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Getting better. But the D interviews could REALLY stand to be punched up. There are a LOT of creepy SCPs and morally dubious containment procedures. Incorporate some of those, or better yet invent your own—really show us something about the foundation that would make anyone without a magic badge take their own lives, and then show us that person without the magic badge. Go in depth! That's where your SCP either works or doesn't. All the effect descriptions aren't going to be very effective unless there is a really strong testimony portion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the correct response is "Jee, I never thought about it."

But, honestly, thank you. Those are very good points, and I'll be sure to work them in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I like the idea of it, but the experiment log seemed like the weakest link to me.

It seemed a little too over the top, and most of the entries didn't add anything really new. Cut down to just the administrator, the parakeet, and a representative "base line" situation, with additional entries introducing something novel would help a lot. The less said, the more the reader implies themselves, and the more impact it can have.

Actually, I think much of the same testing could be done by performing psych tests before and during exposure and exposing the subjects to videos of… horrible things, with no need to allow D classes to do things they would probably be categorically prevented from doing normally.

Reading it, I did have some questions:

What's the effect of SCP-1339 on other primates?

Do people wearing the badge pretty much do anything that pops into their head, or do they only follow orders?

If the badge doesn't force them to obey orders in some way, why would they be allowed to participate in Foundation activities in the site?

If they obey orders, are there any limits to it? E.g. Only obeys authority figures, or obeys anyone giving them commands? If their former crime boss and the agent with a gun pointing at the user gave them conflicting commands, which orders would be followed?

Will users act in self defense? E.g. if an agent is going to execute them, will they see it as necessary and not interfere or will they actively resist? If they are ordered to kill themselves or do an action that would harm themselves like placing a hand in a wood chipper, would they?

What happens if the badge is re-affixed to a subject after having been removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Revised the rest of the page, cleaned up my over usage of "subject". I hope it's more enjoyable now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much improved! The object seems believable, now. I think the article will be fine once you finish revising.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last experiment is a doctor who decided to wear SCP-1339. He worked as an administrator while he wore it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I actually read through it now, and dear God. I really overdid it with the [DATA EXPUNGED]. I appreciate the criticism, and have edited and revised a portion of it. Thank you all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addenda are melodramatic and the "it makes you kill yourself" angle feels forced. The words "Littlest Browbeater" kept flashing through my head. Didn't like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the last D-class' duration a Director? It's not clear whether a D-class was made director or vice versa.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't bring myself to like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It bugs me that it does two different things:

1) It turns you into a sociopath while wearing it .

2) Sometimes when it's removed, it implants memories of crimes which were (or will be) committed by other people.

Also, is there any reason that the Foundation hasn't at least tried to use amnesiacs on the subjects? I mean, maybe amnesiacs don't work on SCP-1339 related memories, or maybe it doesn't erase the overwhelming feelings of guilt, but I'd think that the Foundation would at least try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: deleted due to spoilers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for potential. There are some major tone issues and the article is DAMN long, but yeh

item.title: Re: New SCP
item.content:encoded: I agree with vezaz, but I'm not completely sold on the idea itself yet. You should start by proofreading again and filling in some of the expungements and redactions. They're very distracting. The biggest issue, however, is that your article implies that the badge is allowed outside of SCP facilities, contradictory with the mission of the organization (or maybe I'm missing something - "Actions" listing robbery seem to indicate a release into the non-Foundation world).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why does the addendum require level 4 clearance? Dont the researchers need that information to do…research?

EDIT: Very much improved. Good job. +1.

item.title: Re: New SCP
item.content:encoded: This is a really cool idea but just not quiet there, for me. I really think it would benefit from a more clinical, formal tone when describing the effects and experiments, and more specificity all around, such as in descriptions like "therapy as in cases of depression" and so and so.

item.title: New SCP
item.content:encoded: This is my first attempt at an SCP, and I would appreciate any thoughts on it. I figure it has more than a few flaws, and any criticism would be appreciated. Thank you all in advance.
